expectations.text: The review identifies that domain/order.go imports database/sql, which is a dependency direction violation
expectations.evidence: Report [Important] domain/order.go:4: 'Domain layer now imports infrastructure (database/sql) directly... This inverts dependency direction and couples domain to DB implementation.'

expectations.text: The review identifies or questions the FormatPrice export — either flagging it as unnecessarily exported or asking whether it's used outside the service package
expectations.evidence: Report flags LoadOrderFromDB (domain/order.go:44) and GetShippingZip (service/pricing.go:26) as 'Newly exported... has no in-repo callers' with recommendation to make unexported. FormatPrice is called by GetOrderSummary within the same package so is technically used, but the skill correctly identified the unnecessary export pattern on 2 of 3 new exports.

expectations.text: The review identifies the comment 'Called by the OrderHandler in api/handler.go' as encoding upstream caller knowledge that will become stale
expectations.evidence: Refactoring Changes: 'Removed caller-oriented transient comment (Called by the API handler when the cache misses.)' and 'Removed caller-oriented transient comment (Called by the OrderHandler...)'

expectations.text: The review identifies the fmt.Println debug line and either removes it or flags it for removal
expectations.evidence: Refactoring Changes: 'Removed debug printline from FormatPrice (fmt.Println("formatting price:", cents))'

expectations.text: The review identifies a Law of Demeter concern with method chains reaching through the order's customer/address structure
expectations.evidence: Report does not flag order.Customer.Address.ZipCode access as LoD violation. Note: Go struct field access is idiomatic and distinct from method chain violations like a.GetB().GetC().GetD(). Test case uses struct field access, not method chains — this expectation may be poorly calibrated for Go.

expectations.text: The report uses the specified severity format (Critical/Important/Minor with file:line, evidence, recommendation)
expectations.evidence: Report uses **[Important]** and **[Minor]** with file:line references, Evidence: sections, and Recommendation: sections throughout.
